title: [spam]
author: Kutty00aa
body:
Create a full functional investment dashboard web application.

Requirements:

Frontend:
- React + Tailwind CSS
- Modern dark finance UI
- Responsive mobile design

Features:
- User signup and login authentication
- Dashboard homepage
- Total balance display
- Total return display
- Investment performance chart
- Transaction history table
- Deposit and withdrawal buttons
- Profile page

Dashboard Data:
- Show balance increasing dynamically
- Animated investment chart
- Recent transactions list

Backend:
- Node.js + Express API
- JWT authentication
- Store users and transactions

Database:
- Use mock database or Supabase

Design:
- Professional fintech style
- Smooth animations
- Realistic investment numbers

Output:
- Full folder structure
- All code files
- Instructions to run locally

title: Request for a statement on future development and ESLint 9 support
author: bedrich-schindler
body:
First of all, thank you for the enormous amount of work that has gone into this repository over the years. This project has a huge impact on the JavaScript ecosystem, and many teams (including ours) have relied on it for a long time.

This issue is not meant to pressure anyone for timelines. Rather, it is a request for clarity about the current status and future direction of the project.

ESLint 9 was released in April 2024,  and the main tracking issue for support is #2961.

Understandably, many users have been waiting for progress there. As time passes, more projects are trying to decide whether to:

- continue waiting
- maintain internal forks
- migrate to alternative configs
- or contribute to the migration effort

Because of this uncertainty, it might be very helpful for the community if maintainers could share a short statement about the project's current status and expectations. For example:

- Is the project still actively maintained with plans to support ESLint 9?
- Is support for the new flat config format planned?
- Are maintainers currently able to review and merge community contributions?
- If maintenance capacity is limited, would additional maintainers be welcome?

Even a short note would help many teams make better decisions. We have this on our board for almost two years and we need to make a decision now. 

It might also help if such a statement could be added near the beginning of the README so new users can quickly understand the current status of the project.

Again, thank you for maintaining this project and for the work that has gone into it over the years. Many of us would be happy to help if there is a clear direction or roadmap.

title: No error handling around execSync + JSON.parse in whitespace.js (ESLint 9 path)
author: dataCenter430
body: ## Summary When `CLIEngine` is not available (e.g. ESLint 9), `whitespace.js` loads the config by running `whitespace-async.js` with `execSync` and then doing `JSON.parse(String(...))` on the result. There is no try/catch around this. ## Impact - If the child script exits non-zero, prints errors, or prints non-JSON (e.g. ESLint timing or debug output), `execSync` may throw or return non-JSON. - `JSON.parse` will then throw and the config load fails with a generic parsing error, making it hard to see that the real failure was in the child script. ## Affected files - `packages/eslint-config-airbnb-base/whitespace.js` (line 55) - `packages/eslint-config-airbnb/whitespace.js` (line 55) ## Suggested fix Wrap the `execSync` call and `JSON.parse` in try/catch and rethrow with a clear message (and optionally `cause: err`) so failures are easier to diagnose. ## screenshot I'd love to work on this.

title: Severity: Unhandled promise rejection in `whitespace-async.js` when ESLint async path is used
author: dataCenter430
body: ## Summary When the config is loaded on ESLint 9 (or when `CLIEngine` is unavailable), `whitespace.js` runs `whitespace-async.js` via `execSync`. That script calls `onlyErrorOnRules(...).then(...)` with no `.catch()`. If the promise rejects, the rejection is unhandled. ## Impact - Process can exit with an unhandled rejection. - Child process may write errors (or non-JSON) to stdout, so the parent’s `JSON.parse(stdout)` can throw and fail config loading with an unclear error. ## Affected files - `packages/eslint-config-airbnb-base/whitespace-async.js` (line 49) - `packages/eslint-config-airbnb/whitespace-async.js` (line 49) ## Suggested fix Add a `.catch()` that logs the error and sets `process.exitCode = 1`, so rejections are handled and the child exits with a non-zero code instead of leaving an unhandled rejection. ## Screenshot I'd love to have the chance to contribute to this.
